articles.headline: Aspect Aerospace secures early funding to advance swarm-deployable VLEO satellites
articles.blurb: Aspect Aerospace, a University of South Alabama spin-off, has secured $2.4 million to develop circuit-board-sized spacecraft that could be deployed from space into very low Earth orbit in swarms to monitor the space environment.
articles.takeaway: Circuit-board-sized satellites deployable from carrier spacecraft could dramatically reduce launch costs and enable distributed sensing networks for space domain awareness.

articles.headline: F-35 Crashes in Nevada, Pilot Ejected Safely
articles.blurb: An F-35 went down 25 miles northeast of Indian Springs, inside the NTTR. According to the U.S. Air Force, the pilot ejected safely and was treated for minor injuries. A U.S. Air Force F-35A Lightning II fighter jet crashed during a mission over the Nevada Test & Training Range (NTTR) on Mar. 31,...
articles.takeaway: F-35 ejection systems continue to demonstrate reliability in combat aircraft despite operational losses, underscoring the critical role of pilot safety systems in high-performance fighter operations.

articles.headline: Hawaii Air Tour Operator Suspends Flights After Fatal Crash

articles.headline: How to Assess Safety Risk

articles.takeaway: Organizations must document their rationale for accepting residual risk after implementing controls, creating an auditable decision trail that demonstrates compliance with regulatory expectations.

articles.headline: Creating near-term lunar settlements: lessons from space history
articles.blurb: March 16, 2026, was the 100th anniversary of Robert Goddard’s first flight of a liquid fueled rocket. It reached an altitude of 41 feet. 31 years later, in 1957, Sputnik began a lonely beep as the first satellite in orbit. In 1969, 12 years after Sputnik and 43 years after Goddard’s first flight,...
articles.takeaway: Historical space milestones achieved exponential progress over decades, suggesting lunar settlement timelines require sustained institutional commitment rather than optimistic engineering projections.

articles.headline: WB-57 and Secretive RAT-55 Spotted Rehearsing for NASA’s Artemis Launch
articles.blurb: The secretive U.S. Air Force NT-43A RAT55 was spotted flying with a NASA callsign in one of the areas reserved for the launch of the Artemis II mission, opposite to the one used by the WB-57 Canberra. articles.takeaway: NASA's Artemis II launch will employ specialized atmospheric research aircraft including the rarely-seen RAT-55 to monitor upper-atmosphere conditions during the April 2026 mission.

articles.headline: Artemis 2 fueling underway
articles.blurb: Fueling of the SLS is underway for an April 1 launch attempt of Artemis 2, the first mission to send humans toward the moon in more than 50 years.
articles.takeaway: Artemis 2's fueling milestone signals NASA's readiness to resume lunar human spaceflight after five decades, with implications for commercial space partnerships and aerospace supply chain sustainability.

articles.headline: Boeing's Higher MTOW 787 Dreamliners Cleared By FAA

articles.takeaway: FAA certification of increased 787 MTOW enables airlines to carry higher payload-to-fuel ratios, directly improving route economics and operational flexibility for long-haul operators.

articles.headline: Why the 787-10 uses fewer wheels than the A350-1000
articles.blurb: Why In The World Does The Boeing 787-10 Dreamliner Have Fewer Wheels Than The Airbus A350-1000?At first glance, the landing gear of modern widebody aircraft can look almost identical. Large twin-engine jets sit low on the ground, supported by multiple wheels arranged in similar patterns, and unless...
articles.takeaway: The 787-10's lighter composite structure enables fewer landing gear wheels through advanced weight distribution engineering, reducing maintenance costs and complexity compared to the heavier A350-1000.

articles.headline: Why Do Cargo Carriers Still Avoid Twin-Engine Replacements?
articles.blurb: Older jets can still be highly valuable if they offer the right mix of range, volume, and operating economics.
articles.takeaway: Aging single-aisle freighters remain economically competitive despite twin-engine alternatives due to superior payload-range efficiency and lower acquisition costs for cargo operators.

articles.headline: After three years, Artemis 2 astronauts ready to launch

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Operational efficiency improvements across all aviation layers can simultaneously address capacity constraints and environmental impact, making sustainability a competitive advantage rather than compliance burden.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts
